name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical instrumentation and materials characterization. Expertise in spectroscopy (FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR), chromatography (LC-MS, GC-MS), and thermal analysis (TGA, DSC). Strong background in method development, analytical problem-solving, and cross-functional technical communication. Passionate about helping customers optimize their analytical workflows and achieve reliable results. Seeking to leverage technical expertise and customer-facing skills as an Application Scientist at Waters Corporation.

relevant_bullets: Develop and optimize analytical methods for polymer characterization using FT-IR, TGA, and DSC
Apply statistical experimental design to identify critical process parameters and improve analytical reproducibility
Developed and validated spectroelectrochemical methods combining FT-IR, UV-Vis, and electrochemical analysis
Conducted method development and optimization for materials characterization using multiple analytical techniques
Managed analytical laboratory operations for 15-member research group; implemented equipment tracking and calibration systems improving instrument uptime by 30%
Developed SOPs and trained lab members on analytical instrumentation including FT-IR, UV-Vis, and thermal analysis systems
Developed CVD-based coating processes with comprehensive analytical characterization using FT-IR and ellipsometry
Collaborated with cross-functional engineering teams to communicate analytical findings and design recommendations
Managed analytical instrumentation for research group including FT-IR, UV-Vis, TGA, DSC, and electrochemical systems
Developed training protocols and mentored 15+ researchers on proper analytical techniques and instrument operation
Implemented calibration and maintenance schedules ensuring reliable instrument performance

education.details: Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
